sections.paragraph:
ἐξαφίημι aor. inf. ἐξαφεῖναι 2 Macc 12:24; aor. pass. inf. ἐξαφεθῆναι (ἀφίημι; Soph. et al., in various senses; 2 Macc 12:24; Joseph.; pap; pass. PMerton 79, 8 [II b.c.]) to set free from restraint, let loose, release ἐκέλευσεν ἀυτῷ ἐξαφεθῆναι λέοντα he gave the order to have a lion released against (Paul) AcPl 4:19.

aor. aor. = aorist

inf. inf. = infinitive

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 346
